title: Phase 6 sidecar — attack surface curations + additions
description: Phase-6 LLM persists curation hints + additions at PHASE_END. The Python aggregator (build_threat_model_yaml.py) emits baseline attack_surface[] from .route-inventory.json[routes] (all 112+ routes), then applies these overrides: curations[] filter the noisy baseline to ~20-30 high-signal entries; additions[] adds non-route surfaces (SSH, file watchers, scheduled jobs, cloud events) absent from route-inventory.

curations.description: Filtering hints applied to the baseline route-inventory. When omitted, aggregator emits ALL routes (lower quality but non-breaking).

include_route_ids.description: Allowlist of route_ids to keep from .route-inventory.json[routes][].route_id. When non-empty, all OTHER routes are dropped from attack_surface[].

exclude_route_ids.description: Denylist applied AFTER include_route_ids (or to the full baseline if include is empty). Use for explicit drops (e.g. health-check endpoints).

rationale_by_id.description: Optional per-route rationale rendered as the `notes` field in §5 Attack Surface.

additions.description: Attack surfaces NOT in route-inventory (out-of-band management, scheduled jobs, cloud events). Each entry follows the threat-model.output.schema.yaml attack_surface[] shape.

protocol.description: HTTP / HTTPS / gRPC / SSH / SMTP / MQTT / Cron / FileWatch / etc.
